sections.description: Markdown bodies. Inline claim references like [C3] tie prose to the claims ledger. Required sections per the brief: snapshot, hypotheses, swot, problem_product, traction. Optional sections may be empty strings — their absence is then flagged in gaps, never silently omitted.

hypotheses.description: The explicit why-we-would-invest bullets. Each carries a [prov:] line.

claims.description: The per-claim trust ledger. Every load-bearing assertion in the sections appears here with its evidence and Trust Score. The Trust Score is per claim, never one number for the company.

id.description: C1, C2, ... referenced inline from sections.

authority.description: subject-authority evidence never substantiates its own case: a deck claim supported only by the deck stays tier=claimed no matter how detailed the deck is. Citing subject or agent-derived material as if independent is authority laundering — a named defect.

verification.description: grounded: verbatim support found at an archived source. unverified: search attempted and failed — say where you looked. contradicted: conflicting signals exist; both cited.

quote.description: Verbatim from the signal — never a paraphrase presented as a quote.

contradictions.description: Signal ids or claim ids in tension with this claim. Surfaced before they reach the investor, never smoothed over.

gaps.description: Explicitly flagged missing data — 'Cap table: not disclosed', 'Customer references: unavailable at this stage'. A memo that marks its own gaps is more trustworthy than one that fills them invisibly. Never fabricate.

diligence_log.description: What was checked and what remains open. Every open item names its cheapest decisive check — the concrete instrument, not 'do more research'.

instrument.description: For done: what was run and what it showed. For open: the named cheapest decisive check that would close it.

conditions.description: Milestone gates or tranche conditions, if any — often the Instrument from a forum residual.

rationale.description: The recommendation a human investor acts on within 24 hours. The human is the gate; this is agent-derived input, and says so.

provenance.description: One line per major conclusion, machine-countable: [prov: C3 / traction / sig-014]. Every recommendation traces to the exact data point that drove it.
